You fly by gliding on wind currents. Your maximum gliding distance is limited to your starting height distance rank plus your flight speed rank. You may be able to gain altitude occasionally by catching a thermal or updraft.

–1 cost per rank.

When you heal a subject, you take on their damage conditions yourself. Your Toughness cannot resist this damage.

–1 cost per rank.

The healing you provide is temporary, lasting for only one minute, after which the subject’s damage conditions return.

–1 cost per rank.

You can only leap your maximum distance; you can make shorter leaps only by not using your Leaping effect at all.

Flat –1 point.

You require a medium (like shadows, mirrors, electricity, etc.) to teleport.

–1 cost per rank.

Once you have hit with a Concentration Affliction, as long as you continue to take a standard action each turn to maintain the effect, the target must make a new resistance check against it, with no attack check required.


+1 cost per Power rank.

Some Afflictions may be initially resisted by Dodge, representing the need for quick reaction time or reflexes to avoid the effect. In this case, the later resistance checks to remove the Affliction’s conditions are typically still based on Fortitude or Will.


+0 cost

A Cumulative Affliction adds any further degrees to the existing degrees on the target.


+1 cost per Power rank.

This modifier causes an Affliction to increase incrementally without any effort from you. If the target fails a resistance check to end the Affliction, it not only persists, but increases in effect by one degree!


+2 cost per Power rank.

You get +2 per Accurate rank to attack checks made with it.


Flat 1 point per rank.

An incorporeal being can use an effect on the corporeal world.


When an effect is used against a corporeal target, the effect’s rank is equal to the rank of this extra, up to a maximum of the effect’s full rank.


Flat 1 point per extra rank.

An effect with this extra works on insubstantial targets, in addition to having its normal effect on corporeal targets.


Rank 1 allows the effect to work at half its normal rank; rank 2 allows it to function at full rank.


Flat 1 or 2 points.

This modifier allows effects normally resisted by Fortitude to work on non-living objects (those with no Stamina). If the effect Affects Only Objects, working on objects but not on living creatures, it has a net modifier of +0.


Flat 0 or 1 point

This extra allows you to give someone else use of a personal effect. You must touch the subject as a standard action, and they have control over their use of the effect. If the effect Affects Only Others, and not you, it has a net modifier of +0.


Flat 0 or 1 point

This modifier allows you to “swap-out” the effect for an entire other, alternate, effect!


Flat 1 or 2 points.

This extra allows an effect that normally works on a single target to affect an area. Player must choose the Shape and Range from MnM 3e PHB


No attack check is needed; potential targets are permitted a Dodge resistance check (DC 10 + effect rank) to reduce the effect.


+1 cost per Power rank.

Contagious effects work on both the target and anyone coming into contact with the target. New targets resist the effect normally.


+1 cost per Power rank.

This modifier allows an effect to work on targets in other dimensions. Rank 1 affects one dimension, Rank 2 a related group, Rank 3 any dimension.


Flat 1-3 points.

Each rank of Extended Range doubles all of the effect’s range categories.


Flat 1 point per rank.

Adds some minor additional capability or benefit to a basic effect.


Flat 1 point per rank.

This modifier grants a ranged effect an additional opportunity to hit if it misses.


Flat 1 point per rank.

A defense with this modifier ignores any effect with a resistance difficulty modifier equal to or less than half the Impervious rank.


+1 cost per Power rank.

Each rank of this extra increases the mass rank you can carry or move with the effect by 1.


Flat 1 point per rank.

Effects such as Healing and Regeneration cannot heal the damage caused by an effect with this modifier.


Flat 1 point.

A ranged effect with this modifier can originate from a point other than the user, ignoring cover.


Flat 1-4 points.


• Indirect 1: the effect originates from a fixed point away from you.

• Indirect 2: the effect can come from any point away from you or a fixed point in a fixed direction (notaway from you).

• Indirect 3: The effect can come from any point in a fixed direction (not away from you) or a fixed point in any direction. 

• Indirect 4: The effect can originate from any point and aim in any direction, including towards you (hitting a target in front of you from behind, for example).

An effect with this modifier is an innate part of your nature and unaffected by Nullify.


Flat 1 point.

This modifier makes the result of an effect harder to detect (DC 20 skill check).


Flat 1 point.

Your effect overcomes Impervious Resistance to a degree.


Flat 1 point per rank.

You can use a Precise effect to perform tasks requiring delicacy and fine control.


Flat 1 point.

Each rank of this modifier to a close range effect extends its reach by 5 feet.


Flat 1 point per rank.

Changes an effect’s required action to a reaction, occurring automatically when a triggering event occurs.

+1 or +3 cost per rank.

You can remove conditions caused by a Reversible effect at will as a free action.


Flat 1 point.

You can ricochet or bounce an attack effect off of a solid surface to change its direction.


Each rank in Ricochet allows you to bounce the attack once before it hits. Ricochet may grant a bonus to hit due to surprise, at the GM’s discretion.


Flat 1 point per rank.

An instant duration effect with this modifier affects the target once immediately and then once again on the following round.


+1 cost per Power rank.

A resistible effect with this extra is discriminating, allowing you to decide who is and is not affected by it.


+1 cost per Power rank.

The effect leaves targets asleep whenever it would normally render them incapacitated.


+0 cost per Power rank.

A resistible effect that works on one target can be split between two or more targets.


Each additional rank of this modifier allows the power to split an additional time, so rank 2 allows an effect to split among three targets, then four, and so forth. An effect cannot split to less than 1 rank per target, and cannot apply more than one split to the same target


Flat 1 point per rank.

Subtle effects are not as noticeable. Rank 1 is difficult to notice, Rank 2 is undetectable.


Flat 1-2 points.

Applied to a permanent duration effect, this modifier makes it sustained duration.


+0 cost per Power rank.

You can “set” an instant duration effect to activate under particular circumstances.


You can “set” an instant duration effect with this modifier to activate under particular circumstances, such as in response to a particular danger, after a set amount of time, in response to a particular event, and so forth—chosen when you apply the modifier.


Once chosen, the trigger cannot be changed. The circumstances must be detectable by your senses. You can acquire Senses Limited and Linked to Triggered effects, if desired. Setting the effect requires the same action as using it normally.


A Triggered effect lying in wait may be detected with a Perception check (DC 10 + effect rank) and in some cases disarmed with a successful skill or power check (such as Sleight of Hand, Technology, Nullify or another countering effect) with a DC of (10 + effect rank).


A Triggered effect is good for one use per rank in this modifier. After its last activation, it stops working. You can apply an additional rank of Triggered to have a Variable Trigger, allowing you to change the effect’s trigger each time you set it.


Flat 1 point per rank.

You can change the descriptors of an effect with this modifier, varying them as a free action once per round. With rank 1, you can apply any of a closely related group of descriptors, such as weather, electromagnetic, temperature, and so forth. With rank 2, you can apply any of a broad group, such as any mental, magical, or technological descriptor. The GM decides if a given descriptor is appropriate in conjunction with a particular effect and this modifier


Flat 1-2 points.

You can reflect attacks back at the attacker as a free action. First, you must successfully deflect the attack, then make a normal attack check using your own attack modifier to hit with the reflected attack. It has its normal effect if it hits.


+1 cost per Power rank.

You can redirect attacks you successfully deflect at any target within the attack’s normal range, as Reflect, above. You must have the Reflect extra to take this one.


+1 cost per Power rank.

Your Extra Limbs are merely a projection of your power rather than an extension of your body. Therefore, any harm directed specifically against your Extra Limb(s) has no effect. So, for example, one of your additional limbs could reach into a container of acid or a blast furnace to pull out an object without any harm to you.


+1 cost per Power rank.

You can move underwater as easily as in the air. You have a water speed equal to your Flight rank, minus 2, subject to the usual rules for swimming. You can make Athletics checks to swim as routine checks.


Flat +1 point.

You can heal the fatigued and exhausted conditions as well as damage conditions: DC 10, one degree of success for fatigued, two degrees for exhausted.


However, you take on the removed conditions and cannot use Healing to eliminate your own fatigue (although you can still use hero points to recover from them). If the Healing check fails, you must wait the normal recovery time or use extra effort to try again.


+1 cost per Power rank.

You can stabilize a dying subject as a free action without a check.

Flat +1 point.

Your Healing effect can restore power points removed by Weaken effects with the appropriate descriptors, such as injury, disease, or poison. You restore points equal to your rank to the affected trait(s).


+1 cost per Power rank.

You can return the dead to life. If the subject has been dead for more than a minute, the DC is 10 + 1 per additional minute. If the check fails, you can't try again on that subject.


+1 cost per Power rank.

Your active illusions only require a free action to maintain, rather than a standard action.


+1 cost per Power rank.

Each rank in this extra allows you to have an entirely different character sheet for one of your forms, with the same point total.


Flat +1 point per rank.

You retain your full Strength and related traits (Stamina, etc.) while at your smaller size.


+1 cost per Power rank.

You can use your Shrinking momentum to add to the force of your attacks. You gain a +1 bonus to Damage per 4 ranks of Shrinking.


Flat +1 point.

You can move your created objects as if you had Move Object with a Strength equal to your Create rank.


+1 cost per Power rank.

Your created objects are stationary and remain where they are even if you move away.


Your created objects can hang immobile in the air. They resist being moved with a Strength rank equal to the modifier rank. Unless you have the Tether extra or the Movable extra, you cannot move a stationary created object once it’s placed any more than anyone else can.


+0 cost per rank.

You can create a line or 'tether' of force connecting you to your created object.

Flat +1 point.

You open a portal or gateway between two points as a free action. The portal is five feet across. Anyone stepping through the portal (a move action) is transported. The portal remains open as long as you concentrate, taking a standard action each turn to maintain it


+2 cost per Power rank.

You can teleport, take an action, and then teleport back to your starting point in the same round.

Flat +1 point.

You can change your velocity relative to your starting point when you teleport.


Flat +1 point.

You can change your direction when you teleport.


Flat +1 point.

Teleporting is easy for you, requiring only a move action for any distance.


+1 cost per Power rank.

You choose who perceives your Illusion and who doesn’t.


+1 cost per Power rank.

Close (Touch) -> Ranged (Requires an attack roll; has short/medium/long range).

Ranged -> Perception (No attack roll needed; works as long as you can accurately sense the target).

+1 cost per rank.

• No "One-Hit Wonder" Rule: Standard minions are incapacitated the moment they fail a single resistance check, regardless of the degree of failure. A Heroic duplicate has a full health track and can take multiple bruises or injuries just like you.
  • Action Economy: Heroic duplicates do not have the Dazed condition when summoned. This means they get a full set of actions (Standard, Move, and Free) every round without you needing to apply the "Active" modifier separately, as Heroic already includes it.
  • Critical Hits: Minions cannot score critical hits against non-minions, but Heroic duplicates can, making them much more dangerous in a fight.
  • No Routine Attacks: Enemies can normally take a "routine check" (treating their d20 roll as a 10) to automatically hit a minion. They cannot do this against a Heroic duplicate; they must roll to hit.
